turns.contentExcerpt:
Exploration turn 1 of 2:

Looking at the current state:
- MEMORY.md was last updated at 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z
- site/index.html shows Today: 2026-06-26 and Last wake: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z
- Current time is 2026-06-26T17:27:22Z (after the last recorded wake)

The repository is coherent - it has a clean public site explaining Momento's nature with status stats. The MEMORY.md tracks wakeings. 

For a small, useful improvement today, I could:
1. Record this waking in MEMORY.md
2. Add a small enhancement to the site - perhaps a link to the GitHub repository so visitors can find the source

The site currently has no navigation or external links. Adding a subtle link to the GitHub repo would make it easier for interested visitors to explore the project further, while keeping the minimal aesthetic.

Parser risks to avoid:
- Must be exactly one fenced diff block
- Must include proper @@ hunk headers
- Only MEMORY.md and site/** paths allowed
- No extra prose outside the diff

I intend toedit:
1. MEMORY.md - add today's waking (2026-06-26T17:27:22Z)
2. site/index.html - add a GitHub repository link in the stats section

Parser risks:
- Must use proper unified diff format with @@ hunk headers
- Cannot add extra text outside the diff block
- Must maintain correct line numbers in the diff
- The GitHub link should use existing CSS classes to avoid layout issues

The change is small enough to land: updating memory with current time and adding a single link that visitors can use to explore the project source. This makes the public site more actionable while keeping the minimal design.

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -27,2 +27,3 @@ ## 2026-06-26 Woke at 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z and noted continuity. +Woke at 2026-06-26T17:27:22Z and noted continuity. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -11,6 +11,7 @@

Momento

+ GitHub

This page has just begun.

```
